clinical_trials.title: Tinnitus and Treatment With umPEA-LUT

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Spontaneous resolution in 50-60% of acute tinnitus (<3 months) without treatment; chronic tinnitus (>3 months): only 10-15% spontaneous resolution; habituated tinnitus (not distressing) in 75% at 5 years but sound remains in most
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Cognitive behavioural therapy (CBT): 50-60% reduction in tinnitus-related distress (not sound elimination); tinnitus retraining therapy (TRT): 80% improvement in distress over 18 months; hearing aids for comorbid hearing loss: modest tinnitus reduction; sound therapy (masking): symptom relief without cure
remission.gap_size: Very large — no pharmacological treatment exists; CBT and TRT are evidence-based but massively under-accessed
remission.barrier_detail: No effective pharmacotherapy exists (multiple drug classes tried and failed); sound therapy and CBT are the only evidence-based interventions but trained tinnitus therapists are scarce; most patients are told to 'live with it'; digital CBT for tinnitus (iCBT) emerging as scalable solution
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — Lenire (bimodal neurostimulation device) cleared in some markets; 2022 FDA De Novo cleared for SAINT protocol-based devices; no pharmacotherapy approved; CBT/TRT standard since 1990s
remission.outdated_treatments: Vasodilators (ginkgo biloba, betahistine, nifedipine) prescribed for tinnitus (multiple RCTs negative; no vasoactive mechanism relevant in most tinnitus); hypnotherapy as primary intervention (limited evidence); antidepressants prescribed primarily for tinnitus sound rather than comorbid depression/anxiety
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Bimodal neurostimulation (Lenire — simultaneous auditory + tongue tip stimulation; Phase 3 TENT-A2 positive: 66% significant improvement vs 48% control); transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS) targeting auditory cortex; closed-loop neurofeedback; acoustic coordinated reset neuromodulation (CR neuromodulation)
remission.frontier_signal: Lenire (bimodal stimulation — expanding regulatory clearance); AM-101 (NMDA receptor antagonist, intratympanic — Phase 3 failed primary endpoint globally but some subgroups positive); rTMS; cochlear blood flow targeted therapies; molecular approaches to cochlear hair cell regeneration (Atoh1 gene delivery — may prevent tinnitus post-ototoxic drug)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
